worth_it_explanation: Format choice is one of the few creative levers an auditor can confirm quickly inside Campaign Manager without waiting on data. LinkedIn ties the available formats to the objective, and some formats suit a goal better than the one chosen out of habit, so a quick read of each campaign's objective against its format surfaces a mismatch fast. The same review shows whether a campaign leans on a single format with nothing to test against. Neither finding requires a rebuild. The fix is a new ad or a second format in the same campaign, and it is the kind of change a practitioner can make in the same session.
intro: LinkedIn offers several ad formats, such as single image, video, carousel, document, and message ads, and the objective selected at campaign creation governs which ones are available and which fit the goal. A format chosen for a reason other than the objective, or a campaign that relies on a single format, can hold back the result the objective is meant to reach. These checks confirm that the format suits the objective and that campaigns test more than one format where the objective allows it.

audit_checks.title: The ad format is mismatched to the campaign objective
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A format chosen out of habit rather than for the objective asks the wrong creative to carry the goal, for example a single image ad standing in for a document on a gated-content campaign. The campaign can still deliver, but the format that would suit the objective is left unused, and the result runs below what the objective could reach.

educate.base:
LinkedIn campaigns start with an objective, such as brand awareness, website visits, engagement, video views, or lead generation. The objective the practitioner selects governs which ad formats Campaign Manager makes available, and it also shapes which of those formats suits the goal. The format and the objective are meant to work together, not to be picked independently.

Some pairings are a natural fit. A document ad suits gated content because a member can preview the asset in the feed before submitting a form, so it works well for lead generation. A video ad suits awareness and video views because it holds attention as a member scrolls. A single image ad suits a focused message with one call to action. When the format is chosen for a reason other than the objective, the campaign asks a creative to do a job another format would do better.

A few signs point to a format that does not fit the objective:
  • A lead-generation campaign runs single image ads with an off-platform landing page when a document ad or a lead gen form would capture the lead in feed
  • An awareness campaign runs static single image ads where a video ad would carry the message as a member scrolls
  • A format was reused from a previous campaign with a different objective without revisiting whether it still fits
You may want to read each campaign's objective alongside the format of its ads and confirm the two were chosen together. A format that suits the objective gives the campaign the creative the goal was built around.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business store, a video or carousel ad can demonstrate a product an awareness or engagement objective is built to surface, where a single image ad shows less. You may want to confirm the format matches what the objective is optimizing toward.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead generation is the dominant case on LinkedIn, and the format choice carries it. A document ad or a lead gen form captures the lead in feed, where a single image ad pointing to an off-platform page adds a step. You may want to confirm the format suits an in-feed capture where the objective is lead generation.

investigate.intro: In Campaign Manager, read each campaign's objective and confirm the format of its ads is one the objective is built to use.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager and select the advertising account, then open the campaigns view.
For each campaign, note the objective shown in the campaign's column or its settings, for example brand awareness, website visits, engagement, video views, or lead generation.
Open the campaign and review the format of the ads it runs, such as single image, video, carousel, document, or message.

Compare the format against the objective. Record any campaign whose format was chosen for a reason other than the objective, such as a lead-generation campaign running single image ads to an off-platform page where a document or lead gen form would capture the lead in feed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per campaign, the objective and the format of its ads. A campaign whose format is one the objective is built to use passes. A campaign whose format was chosen for a reason other than the objective is a fail recorded with the campaign name, the objective, and the format that would suit it better.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the objective and ad format for each campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that some campaigns on {{site_name}} run an ad format that does not fit their objective, such as a single image ad on a lead-generation campaign where a document ad or a lead gen form would capture the lead in feed. LinkedIn ties the available formats to the objective, and a format chosen out of habit asks the wrong creative to carry the goal, so the result runs below what the objective could reach. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing each campaign's objective alongside its format and rebuilding the ad in a format the objective is built to use, for example a document or video ad in place of a static image where the goal calls for it.

audit_checks.title: Campaigns run a single ad format with no diversification
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A campaign that runs only single image ads forgoes formats that reach members in a different way, such as video, carousel, and document, and it gives the practitioner no read on which format the audience responds to. The campaign can still deliver, but it learns nothing about format and leaves the reach a second format would add on the table.

educate.base:
LinkedIn supports more than one format under most objectives, including single image, video, carousel, and document ads. A campaign is not required to use more than one, and many run on single image ads alone because that format is the quickest to produce.

Running a single format has two costs. Each format reaches members in a different way. A video holds attention as a member scrolls, a carousel lets a member move through several frames, and a document lets a member preview an asset in feed. A campaign that runs only single image ads reaches members in one way and forgoes the others. The second cost is that the campaign produces no read on format. With one format in the campaign, there is nothing to compare, so the practitioner cannot tell whether the audience would respond more to a video or a document.

A few signs point to a campaign that runs a single format:
  • Every ad in the campaign is the same format, most often single image
  • The campaign has run long enough to add a second format but has not
  • The objective allows more than one format, yet only one is in use
You may want to confirm whether each campaign tests more than one format where the objective allows it. A second format both widens reach and gives the campaign something to compare, so the next round of creative can lean toward what worked.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered or business-to-business store, a single image ad shows the product in one frame, where a carousel or video can show more of it. You may want to confirm the campaign tests a second format so it learns which one the audience responds to.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead generation is the dominant case on LinkedIn, and a single image ad is often the only format in the campaign. You may want to confirm a document ad or a video runs alongside it, both to widen reach and to show which format brings the lead in at a lower cost.

investigate.intro: In Campaign Manager, review the ads in each campaign and confirm whether more than one format is in use where the objective allows it.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open Campaign Manager, select the advertising account, and open the campaigns view.
Open each campaign and list the ads it runs, noting the format of each, such as single image, video, carousel, or document.
Check the campaign's objective to confirm which formats it allows since not every objective supports every format.
Record any campaign that runs a single format where the objective allows more than one, along with the format in use.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per campaign, the formats in use and whether the objective allows more than one. A campaign that runs more than one format, or whose objective allows only one, passes. A campaign that runs a single format where the objective allows more is a fail recorded with the campaign name and the format in use.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the ad formats in use for each campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that several campaigns on {{site_name}} run a single ad format, most often single image, where the objective allows more than one. A single format reaches members in one way and gives the campaign nothing to compare, so it cannot show whether the audience would respond more to a video, a carousel, or a document. {{voice_recommend}} adding a second format to these campaigns where the objective supports it, which both widens reach and gives the next round of creative a read on what the audience responds to.
